facts.fact: The insurer is Guardian Family Protection Corp.
The policy number is GFP-WL-2024-8847.
The effective date is February 1, 2024.
The policy type is Participating Whole Life.
The insured's name is Margaret Rose Chen.
Margaret Rose Chen's date of birth is July 8, 1978.
Margaret Rose Chen's issue age is 45.
Margaret Rose Chen's risk class is Standard Non-Tobacco.
The face amount is $250,000.
The annual premium is $3,240.
The payment frequency is Annual.
The premium payment period is to age 100.
The maturity date is age 100, which corresponds to July 8, 2078.
The primary beneficiary is David Chen (Husband) at 100%.
The contingent beneficiary is Chen Family Trust at 100%.
Cash Surrender Value is defined as the amount available if the policy is surrendered, equal to the cash value minus any outstanding loans and surrender charges.
Dividend is defined as a return of premium that may be declared annually by the Board of Directors based on company experience.
Paid-Up Insurance is defined as a reduced death benefit with no further premiums required.
Policy Loan is defined as a loan made by the insurer using the cash value as collateral.
This policy builds cash value according to the guaranteed schedule shown in the policy values table.
Cash value grows on a tax-deferred basis.
The guaranteed cash value at the end of Year 10 is $28,450.
The guaranteed cash value at the end of Year 20 is $71,200.
Actual cash value may be higher than guaranteed due to dividend credits.
This is a participating policy.
Annual dividends may be declared based on the insurer's mortality, investment, and expense experience.
Dividends are not guaranteed.
Dividend options include: Cash Payment, Premium Reduction, Accumulate at Interest, and Paid-Up Additions.
The current dividend option selected is Paid-Up Additions.
The policy owner may borrow against the cash value after the policy has been in force for one full year.
The maximum policy loan amount is 90% of the current cash surrender value.
The loan interest rate is 5% per annum, compounded annually.
Loan interest may be paid annually or added to the loan balance.
If total indebtedness exceeds the cash value, the policy will terminate unless sufficient payment is made within 31 days of notice.
Outstanding loans and accrued interest will be deducted from the death benefit or cash surrender value.
Upon receipt of proof of death, the insurer will pay the beneficiary: Face Amount + Paid-Up Additions - Policy Loans - Loan Interest.
Death benefit payment will be made within 7 days of receiving complete claim documentation.
Non-forfeiture options are available after the policy has been in force for three years.
The Cash Surrender non-forfeiture option allows the policy owner to receive the cash surrender value and terminate the policy.
The Reduced Paid-Up Insurance non-forfeiture option continues coverage for a reduced death benefit with no further premiums required.
The Reduced Paid-Up Insurance amount is based on cash value and the insured's attained age.
The Extended Term Insurance non-forfeiture option continues the full face amount for a limited period based on cash value with no further premiums.
If no non-forfeiture election is made within 60 days of lapse, Extended Term Insurance will automatically apply.
The policy owner may reinstate the policy within 3 years of lapse.
Reinstatement requires providing evidence of insurability satisfactory to the insurer.
Reinstatement requires paying all overdue premiums with interest at 6% per annum.
Reinstatement requires repaying or reinstating any policy loans with interest.
If the insured dies by suicide within two years from the issue date, the insurer will pay only the premiums paid, without interest.
If the insured's age was misstated, the insurer will adjust the face amount to what the premium would have purchased at the correct age.
The policy owner may cancel the policy within 30 days of receipt for a full refund of premiums paid.
The insurer may contest the policy only within the first two years while the insured is living.
The insurer may contest the policy only for material misrepresentation in the application.
The policy was issued at Boston, Massachusetts.
The authorized signatory is the President of Guardian Family Protection Corp.
The policy was signed on February 1, 2024.
David Chen's relationship to the insured is Husband.
The Paid-Up Additions dividend option purchases additional insurance using dividends.
The Cash Payment dividend option pays the dividend directly to the policy owner.
The Premium Reduction dividend option applies dividends to reduce the next premium.
The Accumulate at Interest dividend option leaves dividends with the insurer to accumulate.
The policy number is GFP-WL-2024-8847.
The policy is a whole life policy, meaning coverage lasts for the insured's entire life to age 100.
The premium payment period extends to age 100 (July 8, 2078).
The insurer's obligation to pay death benefit is reduced by paid-up additions only if loans are subtracted.
The free look period is 30 days from receipt of the policy.
The reinstatement interest rate on overdue premiums is 6% per annum.
The suicide exclusion period is two years from the issue date.
The contestability period is two years from the issue date while the insured is living.
The automatic non-forfeiture option if no election is made within 60 days is Extended Term Insurance.
The policy loan interest rate is 5% per annum compounded annually.
The maximum loan is 90% of current cash surrender value.
The termination notice period for indebtedness exceeding cash value is 31 days.
